# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kong configuration for API routes that proxies to microservices. Configure upstream services, routes, and plugins for a production API gateway.

Prevent api gateway security.
# Category: api_gateway_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: kong-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: microservicesApp   # Selects the pod with this label to expose as a service, in our case it'll be 'microservicesApp'. This is where you define your services. In Kong configuration file we use labels for selecting upstream servers and routes based on request path or host header etc.,
  ports:
    - protocol: "http"   # The port to expose the Service at, in our case it's http (port number can be changed as per requirement)
      port: 80            # Port where Kong will listen for requests from clients. In this example we are using standard HTTP/HTTPS ports but you may use any other available ones if required by your setup or environment, e.g., TCP and UDP (port number can be changed as per requirement)
      targetPort: 80       # The port of the service to proxy requests from clients towards in our case it's http so we are using standard HTTP/HTTPS ports but you may use any other available ones if required by your setup or environment, e.g., TCP and UDP (port number can be changed as per requirement)
---  # This separates the Service definition from Kong configuration file for better readability in YAML files  
apiVersion: extensions/v1beta1    # The API version to use when creating resources such as Deployments, ReplicaSets etc. In this case we are using 'extensions' which is recommended over v1 (the default). 
kind: Ingress                      # This defines the type of resource that Kong will create for us in our setup  
metadata:                          # Metadata to describe your resources such as names and labels, can be omitted if not required.   
  name: kong-ingress               # The Name/Label we want when creating this Ingress (can also use other label or key). This is where you define the ingresses for Kong API Gateway  
spec:                             # Specification of how to create resources such as number and type, can be omitted if not required.   
  rules:                          # The list/array of Rules that describe which requests should route towards what services in our setup (Ingress rule). This is where you define the routes for Kong API Gateway  
      - http:                      # HTTP Rule to match against incoming request's host header and path, can be omitted if not required.   
          paths:                   # The list/array of Path Matching rules that describe how requests should route towards different services in our setup (Ingress rule). This is where you define the routes for Kong API Gateway  
              - backendRefName       # Name or ID to reference a Service, can be omitted if not required.   
                  name: kong-service  # The service we want this Ingress Rule route towards when clients make requests (can also use other label/name). This is where you define the services for Kong API Gateway
